raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Legislation has been signed granting tribal law enforcement agencies and tribal courts access to a national data network used to investigate crimes.",
    "The access provided by this legislation aims to assist tribes in keeping their communities safe and investigating crimes related to missing and murdered Native Americans.",
    "California has the fifth largest caseload of missing and murdered Indigenous women and people.",
    "Nearly one-half of all Indigenous women have been sexually assaulted, beaten, or stalked by an intimate partner.",
    "Without access to the California Law Enforcement Telecommunications System (CLETS), tribal courts and tribal law enforcement are unable to enter domestic violence protective orders or share and update criminal and missing record information.",
    "The lack of CLETS access puts tribal communities at a disadvantage and allows criminal perpetrators to escape justice.",
    "The legislation aims to improve public safety on and off tribal lands by facilitating greater collaboration between tribal police and tribal courts.",
    "Access to CLETS will empower tribal governments and courts to secure their own public safety.",
    "AB 44 is positioned to improve public safety for all people in California."
  ]
}
```
